description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who is testing that yesterday’s
backup files can be “restored” through the creation of symbolic links.

1.  An *original* backup file already exists at  
    /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini  

2.  Your job is to create a *symbolic link* that represents the “restored”
    copy of that file.  The link must be created at  
    /home/user/restore/settings.ini

3.  After making the link, you must create a one-line log file named  
    /home/user/restore/restore_link_test.log  
    whose single line **must be exactly** (no extra spaces or line-breaks):

    /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini

    This line is the absolute path produced by calling  
    readlink -f /home/user/restore/settings.ini

4.  Automation will verify two things:
    • The path /home/user/restore/settings.ini is a *symbolic link* whose
      target is /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini.  
    • The file /home/user/restore/restore_link_test.log exists and its
      complete contents are exactly the single line shown in step 3, ending
      with a single Unix newline (`\n`).

Keep the work to one concise terminal line: create the symbolic link and the
log file in a single command line (commands may be chained with `&&`
or `;`).  No other files should be modified or created.
truth:
Initial machine state before the agent begins:

Directory structure and file contents supplied in the exercise image:
---------------------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/backup/2023-10-10/            (0755)
/home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini (0644) with **exact** contents:
    [General]
    version=1
    (file ends with a single Unix newline)

/home/user/restore/              DOES **NOT** exist yet

Verification the grader will perform after the agent finishes:
--------------------------------------------------------------
1. stat -c "%F" /home/user/restore/settings.ini  → must output "symbolic link"
2. readlink -f /home/user/restore/settings.ini   → must output exactly
      /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini
3. cat /home/user/restore/restore_link_test.log  → must output exactly
      /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini\n
4. File /home/user/backup/2023-10-10/settings.ini must remain unmodified.

No other files under /home/user/ should have been added, removed, or changed.
